name: Pasta with Asiago Cheese
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds fresh egg and spinach pasta, see tip
1 1/2 cups Asiago Cheese, cut into small cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly until bubbly.
3. Gradually whisk in cream, nutmeg, and salt to taste until smooth and thickened.
4. Stir in the grated Asiago cheese until melted and well combined.
5. Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook until al dente (slightly chewy), about 8-10 minutes, or according to package instructions.
6. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta.
7. Return the drained pasta to the saucepan with the cheese sauce. Toss gently to combine.
8. Add red pepper slices and adjust seasoning as needed.
9. Garnish with parsley and black pepper to taste.
[MC-Per Serving: 742 cals, 43g total fat (52% of daily value)]
TIP: This recipe makes enough sauce for 1 to 1+1/2 pounds of pasta.
VARIATIONS: This dish is versatile and can be served as an entree with shrimp or scallops added. It pairs well with veal or chicken.
